does: This paper creates a multimodal ethical database by collecting human feedback on the ethicality of AI system responses involving both text and images, then develops automated classifiers to evaluate whether multimodal AI outputs adhere to ethical standards. The authors train a RoBERTa-large classifier and a multilayer perceptron on this curated database to predict the ethical quality of generative system responses. The core contribution is extending ethical evaluation beyond language-only models to the multimodal domain where systems like DALL-E generate images paired with text, addressing a gap where most AI alignment work has focused narrowly on text-based systems.
connects: This work builds on the broader AI alignment field that seeks to make AI systems reflect human values and preferences, extending recent approaches developed for language models like ChatGPT to the multimodal setting. It responds to growing concerns about the societal impact of generative AI systems across diverse applications from creative tasks to mental health support. The paper draws on techniques from human feedback collection and supervised learning for value specification, connecting to prior work on using human preferences to guide AI behavior and building on classifier-based approaches for automatic assessment of system outputs.
enables: This framework establishes foundations for automated ethical auditing of multimodal generative systems before deployment, potentially enabling more responsible development and deployment of text-image models. The ethicality classifiers could support real-time filtering of problematic outputs, support developers in identifying failure modes, or provide feedback signals for fine-tuning generative models toward more ethical behavior. The multimodal ethical database itself becomes a benchmark resource for the community, enabling future research into value alignment for vision-language models and supporting the development of more sophisticated approaches to detect harmful cross-modal interactions that might emerge from text-image combinations.
